body:
{
  "title": "Anchor All-Hands Q2 2027",
  "compiled_truth": "The Q2 2027 all-hands for [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor) was held on July 3rd, 2027, at the company's primary office with remote dial-in for the distributed team. All eight current full-time employees attended. [Seren Voss](people/seren-voss) opened the meeting with a recap of Q2 milestones: three new design partners onboarded, the deterministic pipeline hitting sub-millisecond latency on benchmark payloads, and the close of the bridge round that extended runway through Q1 2028. Seren was candid about what didn't go well — the Fieldspar integration stalled due to schema mismatches, and hiring for a second infrastructure engineer fell through twice.\n\n[Dani Osei](people/dani-osei) presented the engineering OKR review. The team shipped v0.9 of the single-pass primitives library on schedule, though test coverage on the authentication layer lagged behind target (72% vs. 85% goal). Dani flagged that [Miro Bergmann](people/miro-bergmann) had essentially carried the compression refactor solo and that the workload distribution needed attention heading into Q3. Miro, characteristically quiet, nodded and said he'd document the new module boundaries so onboarding a second engineer would be smoother.\n\n[Tal Kimura](people/tal-kimura) walked through the go-to-market numbers. Pipeline stood at fourteen qualified leads, up from eight at end of Q1. Conversion remained the bottleneck — only two of those had moved to paid pilots. Tal proposed a revised pricing structure that bundles transmission guarantees with the core compression offering, which sparked a lengthy back-and-forth with [Nova Mensah](people/nova-mensah) about whether that muddied the product narrative. No resolution was reached; Seren asked them to bring a joint proposal to the next leadership sync.\n\n[Paz Santos](people/paz-santos) gave a brief update on compliance readiness — SOC 2 Type I audit is on track for September, and she's been coordinating with outside counsel on data residency language for the European prospects. [Ari Leclerc](people/ari-leclerc) demo'd the new observability dashboard, which gives design partners real-time visibility into pipeline throughput. The demo went well though Ari admitted the alerting module still had some rough edges.\n\n[Kael Petrov](people/kael-petrov) closed with a short retro on team health. Morale was generally high but fatigue was creeping in — multiple people mentioned feeling the weight of a small team moving fast. Kael suggested instituting a rotating \"deep work\" day where no meetings are scheduled. The room agreed unanimously, which itself was notable. Overall, the meeting ran about 90 minutes, a bit long for an all-hands, but the energy was good and the candor felt genuine.",
  "timeline": [
    "- **2027-04-01** | Q2 OKRs formally set during leadership sync; engineering targets v0.9 release and 85% test coverage on auth layer.",
    "- **2027-04-18** | First of three new design partners signs LOI after demo by [Tal Kimura](people/tal-kimura).",
    "- **2027-05-07** | [Miro Bergmann](people/miro-bergmann) begins solo compression refactor sprint, estimated at 4 weeks.",
    "- **2027-05-22** | Bridge round closes, extending [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor) runway through Q1 2028.",
    "- **2027-06-10** | v0.9 of single-pass primitives library ships to design partners.",
    "- **2027-06-19** | Fieldspar integration paused due to unresolved schema mismatch issues; [Dani Osei](people/dani-osei) flags in engineering standup.",
    "- **2027-06-27** | [Ari Leclerc](people/ari-leclerc) completes first working build of observability dashboard.",
    "- **2027-07-03** | Q2 all-hands held; full team attends. OKRs reviewed, roadmap discussed, deep-work day policy adopted.",
    "- **2027-07-14** | [Nova Mensah](people/nova-mensah) and [Tal Kimura](people/tal-kimura) scheduled to present joint bundling proposal at leadership sync.",
    "- **2027-09-15** | Target date for SOC 2 Type I audit completion, led by [Paz Santos](people/paz-santos)."
  ]
}
